hard_stops.logic: MEP routing violates code-required clearances — NEC 110.26 electrical panel access (36" deep, 30" wide, clear to ceiling), mechanical service access, plumbing cleanout access, or NFPA 13 sprinkler head clearances (18" minimum below deflector)
MEP penetration through a fire-rated assembly (wall, floor, ceiling) without documented UL-listed firestopping — life-safety code violation
project is in seismic design category C-F and MEP systems (equipment, ductwork, piping, conduit) are missing required seismic bracing per ASCE 7 and IBC

soft_flags.logic: clash identified more than 14 days ago remains unresolved — likely schedule impact
MEP submittal was rejected and resubmission has not been received — may delay procurement and installation
commissioning functional performance testing incomplete and substantial completion is within 30 days
combined MEP trade space requirements exceed available ceiling depth in a zone — coordination meeting required
MEP equipment with long lead time (>8 weeks) has not been ordered and the installation date is approaching
MEP system does not meet ASHRAE 90.1 or IECC energy code requirements (insulation, efficiency, controls, lighting power density)
